children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: Not later than 60 days after , the Secretary of Labor shall establish an Advisory Committee on Increasing Competitive Integrated Employment for Individuals with Disabilities (referred to in this section as the “Committee”).July 22, 2014

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: The Secretary of Labor shall appoint the members of the Committee described in subsection (c)(6), in accordance with subsection (c).

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: 2 Vacancies
children.text: Any vacancy in the Committee shall not affect its powers, but shall be filled in the same manner, in accordance with the same paragraph of subsection (c), as the original appointment or designation was made.
the Assistant Secretary for Disability Employment Policy, the Assistant Secretary for Employment and Training, and the Administrator of the Wage and Hour Division, of the Department of Labor;
the Commissioner of the Administration on Intellectual and Developmental Disabilities, or the Commissioner’s designee;
the Director of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services of the Department of Health and Human Services, or the Director’s designee;
the Commissioner of Social Security, or the Commissioner’s designee;
the Commissioner of the Rehabilitation Services Administration, or the Commissioner’s designee; and

children.label: A
children.text: self-advocates for individuals with intellectual or developmental disabilities;

children.label: B
children.text: providers of employment services, including those that employ individuals with intellectual or developmental disabilities in competitive integrated employment;

children.label: C
children.text: representatives of national disability advocacy organizations for adults with intellectual or developmental disabilities;

children.label: D
children.text: experts with a background in academia or research and expertise in employment and wage policy issues for individuals with intellectual or developmental disabilities;

children.label: E
children.text: representatives from the employer community or national employer organizations; and

children.label: F
children.text: other individuals or representatives of organizations with expertise on increasing opportunities for competitive integrated employment for individuals with disabilities.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: d Chairperson
children.text: The Committee shall elect a Chairperson of the Committee from among the appointed members of the Committee.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: e Meetings
children.text: The Committee shall meet at the call of the Chairperson, but not less than 8 times.
ways to increase the employment opportunities for individuals with intellectual or developmental disabilities or other individuals with significant disabilities in competitive integrated employment;

the use of the certificate program carried out under for the employment of individuals with intellectual or developmental disabilities, or other individuals with significant disabilities; andsection 214(c) of this title
ways to improve oversight of the use of such certificates.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: 1 Travel expenses
children.text: The members of the Committee shall not receive compensation for the performance of services for the Committee, but shall be allowed reasonable travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for employees of agencies under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5, while away from their homes or regular places of business in the performance of services for the Committee. Notwithstanding , the Secretary may accept the voluntary and uncompensated services of members of the Committee.section 1342 of title 31

children.heading: Staff
children.label: 2 Staff
children.text: The Secretary of Labor may designate such personnel as may be necessary to enable the Committee to perform its duties.

children.heading: Detail of Government employees
children.label: 3 Detail of Government employees
children.text: Any Federal Government employee, with the approval of the head of the appropriate Federal agency, may be detailed to the Committee without reimbursement, and such detail shall be without interruption or loss of civil service status or privilege.

children.heading: Facilities, equipment, and services
children.label: 4 Facilities, equipment, and services
children.text: The Secretary of Labor shall make available to the Committee, under such arrangements as may be appropriate, necessary equipment, supplies, and services.

children.label: A
children.text: an interim report that summarizes the progress of the Committee, along with any interim findings, conclusions, and recommendations as described in subsection (f); and

children.label: B
children.text: a final report that states final findings, conclusions, and recommendations as described in subsection (f).

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of the interim report, not later than 1 year after the date on which the Committee is established under subsection (a); and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of the final report, not later than 2 years after the date on which the Committee is established under subsection (a).

children.heading: Termination
children.label: i Termination
children.text: The Committee shall terminate on the day after the date on which the Committee submits the final report.
